title: Market Brief — Friday, March 27, 2026
summary: The BEP LLMflation Index sits at 21.4, down 79% from the GPT-4 launch baseline of 100. Frontier inference continues to deflate — but the deflation is uneven. The premium tier is holding pricing power while the commodity floor races toward zero.
paragraphs: The BEP LLMflation Index sits at 21.4, down 79% from the GPT-4 launch baseline of 100. Frontier inference continues to deflate — but the deflation is uneven. The premium tier is holding pricing power while the commodity floor races toward zero.

Tracking 18 frontier models today. The price spread stands at 211x — DeepSeek: DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.38/M output versus OpenAI: o3 Pro at $80/M. This isn't convergence — it's bifurcation. The market is segmenting into distinct tiers exactly as Jensen mapped at GTC.
GPU spot market: H200 is the tightest at 18% availability. This is a supply constraint signal — demand exceeds deployed capacity on the spot market. 50 total offerings tracked across Vast.ai.

At current spot rates, the cheapest path to token production is H100 at $1.47/hr — roughly $4.29/M tokens for a 70B model. At Premium tier pricing ($45/M), that's ~90% gross margin. At High tier ($6/M), it's barely break-even. The hardware generation you're on determines whether inference is a business or a charity.

Bottom line: The token economy continues to bifurcate. Commodity inference deflates toward zero while premium reasoning and agentic workloads command 50-200x premiums. The investment signal is in the spread, not the average. Track which hardware generation can profitably serve which tier — that's where capital allocation decisions live.
bullets: OpenAI flagship: OpenAI: o3 Pro at $80/M output
Anthropic flagship: Anthropic: Claude Opus 4.6 at $25/M output
DeepSeek floor: DeepSeek: DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.38/M output — sub-dollar inference is here
